title: ca-codex hooks.json drops the dual interpreter registration — a python interpreter miss makes every Codex gate silently fail open
evidence: ca registers every hook TWICE (`python3 <script>` plus `python3 -c "" || python <script>`) precisely because, per _hooklib's header, 'Stock Windows often has no real python3 (the Microsoft Store stub exits 9009), which would make every gate fail OPEN'. ca-codex registers each entry ONCE with `command: python3 …` / `commandWindows: python …`. On Windows, `python` can itself be the Store alias stub (exits 9009); on a POSIX box with only `python`, `python3` fails outright. In either case the single registration has no fallback probe, and a hook command that errors is a non-blocking failure — the gates evaporate with no signal, the exact cold-install hazard the ca cold-install matrix (test_hooks_cold_install.py) exists to prevent. No equivalent Codex-side matrix test exists in .github/scripts/test_codex_adapter.py's scope.
impact: Silent full fail-open of the Codex enforcement surface on stock machines without a working interpreter behind the single registered name — the plugin appears installed but is inert. Confidence tempered: Codex's own hook-failure semantics (fail open vs surfaced error) are not verifiable from this repo.
recommendation: Either replicate the dual-registration probe pattern in ca-codex/hooks.json (if Codex executes multiple entries per matcher the way Claude Code does), or verify Codex surfaces a failed hook command loudly and document that as the accepted contract; add a Codex leg to the cold-install matrix.
acceptance_criteria: A Codex install on a machine where the registered interpreter name is a stub either still blocks via the fallback or produces a loud, user-visible error — never a silent allow
The behavior is covered by a cold-install-style test
